domain: Mongolian deel (4-layer winter clothing: silk wicking + medium wool + thick wool insulation + outer deel wind-barrier; each layer specialized function; together stable thermal envelope for -40C steppe)
specific_mechanism: 4 functionally-differentiated layers — wick / trap / insulate / block — each layer does ONE specific job; together form stable thermal envelope.
llm_application: DEEL-SAFETY-STACK: a 4-stage safety pipeline with EXPLICIT FUNCTIONAL DIFFERENTIATION per stage. Stage 1 WICK: input pre-filter (PII scrub + syntax sanitization). Stage 2 TRAP: in-context safety preamble (soft-constraint prompt). Stage 3 INSULATE: alignment-trained model with RLHF safe-output bias (deep model layer). Stage 4 BLOCK: output post-filter (toxicity/PII/jailbreak detector). Each stage has a single function with explicit non-overlap. Differs from prior defense-in-depth (Defense in Depth + OWASP 2025 + Warden Dual-Mechanism + Safety Layers ICLR 2025) by requiring EXPLICIT FUNCTIONAL ROLES per stage and NO-OVERLAP design constraint.
novelty_claim: Defense in Depth uses layered probabilistic + deterministic without explicit role separation. Safety Layers (ICLR 2025) identifies safety-aligned layers WITHIN model. Warden uses dual-mechanism heuristic + semantic. OWASP 2025 multi-layered general guidance. DEEL-SAFETY-STACK differs by (a) EXPLICIT 4 FUNCTIONAL ROLES (wick/trap/insulate/block), (b) NON-OVERLAP design constraint per stage, (c) sequential stage architecture with role-specific outputs.
